metadata.seed: I would like to work on my avoidance behavior. I actually have an opportunity which I was looking for from a very long time to give a presentation. And now that I have this opportunity in front of me, I'm trying to avoid it.

I guess I always see myself through this really harsh lens, and it makes it hard to even try.

I wonder if I’ll ever really feel different or if this is just how I’ll always see myself. It’s frustrating, because I want to believe I can change, but it’s like there’s this wall I can’t break through.

patient.query: I guess it would help to talk about it more. Sometimes it just feels like I'm stuck in this loop, and I can’t see a way out. It’s like I want to push through, but then I get overwhelmed, and I just retreat back to what feels safe, even if it’s not really helping. I don’t know if I even know what breaking through would look like.

patient.query: Yeah, I definitely feel trapped. It’s like there’s this pressure to be perfect, and every time I feel like I might fall short, I just... freeze. The anxiety feels like this weight on my chest, like I can’t breathe properly. When I think about collapsing, it’s more about the fear of being exposed, like everyone will see that I’m not as capable as they think I am. It’s really scary to imagine that.

automatic_thoughts: everyone will see that I’m not as capable

patient.query: I guess it’s just how I’ve always felt, like there’s this expectation that I have to be perfect. I don’t want to disappoint anyone or have them think less of me. I’ve seen how others react when someone makes a mistake, and I just... I don’t want to be that person. It feels like I have to be strong and put together, or I’ll just be a burden. It’s heavy to carry that around.

automatic_thoughts: I don't want to disappoint anyone
I have to be strong and put together

It feels like there’s this standard I need to meet, and when I don’t, it’s almost like I’ve failed in some way, you know? But deep down, I know it’s unrealistic. I just don’t know how to let go of that pressure. It’s like I’ve built my whole identity around it.
